steps.title: The minimum weight decreased by 12 pounds
steps.content: The horse with the lowest recorded weight actually weighs 12 pounds less than recorded. This is the only change in the data.

steps.title: The median is position-based, not value-dependent
steps.content:
With 17 horses, the median is the 9th value when the data is sorted in ascending order.

The horse with the lowest weight is in position 1. When we decrease its weight by 12 pounds, it remains in position 1-it is still the lowest weight.

The 9th position (where the median value sits) does not change, so the median itself does not change.

steps.title: Mean, range, and standard deviation all depend on values
steps.content:
• Mean depends on the sum of all weights. Decreasing one weight by 12 decreases the sum by 12, so the mean decreases.
  • Range = maximum - minimum. Decreasing the minimum by 12 increases the range by 12.
  • Standard deviation measures the spread of data around the mean. Both the mean and the spread change, so the standard deviation changes.
Only the median remains unchanged. That confirms B is correct.
